metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claudecode hits a quota / rate limit naturally when the Anthropic API returns HTTP 429. The TUI surfaces the error inline (assistant message text 'rate_limit_error' / 'quota exceeded') and the turn ends cleanly without a successful `end_turn` — claude's request finishes, the input prompt reappears, and the session sits in `ready` waiting for the user to either retry or wait for quota reset.

The spec's three Expected: bullets:

1. `working → ready`. Observable: the daemon's state classifier sees the failed turn complete (no stuck working state) and transitions back to ready when claude's input prompt reappears.
2. State reaches `ready` (not stuck in `working`). Same observable.
3. Subsequent user prompts may not produce a new turn until budget resets — state stays `ready`. Observable: the second prompt either hits 429 again (state goes working briefly then back to ready) or claude refuses to send the request (state stays in ready). Either way no waiting state, no stuck working.

**Recording approach.** The previous 2026-05-17 assessment marked this `applicable: false` because triggering a real 5h-subscription cap inside a recording would burn the maintainer's actual quota. Today the recipe uses a mocked Anthropic endpoint (`tools/agent-onboarding/recording/mock-anthropic-429/main.go`) that returns 200 + a valid end_turn streaming response on the first /v1/messages request and HTTP 429 on the second. claude is launched with `--bare` plus `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-mock` + `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` so it bypasses OAuth/keychain and talks to the mock. Result: the second turn deterministically reproduces the quota-exhausted code path without any real billing.

**Why `--bare` is necessary.** Without `--bare`, claude reads OAuth tokens from the keychain first and ignores `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` (per the binary's own `--bare` help text: 'Anthropic auth is strictly ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper via --settings (OAuth and keychain are never read).'). The bespoke driver at `tools/agent-onboarding/recording/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh` launches claude under `--bare` because the standard `drive-claudecode-interactive.sh` does not pass it.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The specific HTTP status code (429 vs other failure modes) is invisible — claude's transcript records an error event, but irrlicht's parser treats all turn-failure shapes as 'turn ended abnormally → ready'. That conflates 429 with 500 / 503 / network errors. The spec doesn't require discriminating; only that state lands cleanly in ready.
caveats: Recording uses a mocked Anthropic endpoint that 429s on the second request. The real 5h subscription quota path is the same shape but harder to trigger inside a recording without burning real money.

claude must be launched with --bare so it reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL from env. The standard driver (drive-claudecode-interactive.sh) does not pass --bare; this scenario uses a bespoke recorder at tools/agent-onboarding/recording/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh.
irrlicht's daemon parser doesn't discriminate 429 from other turn-failure shapes — the spec is satisfied by 'state lands in ready', which is observable regardless of failure code.

sources.note: Confirms ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL env vars; claude --bare semantics (OAuth/keychain bypassed).
Minimal Anthropic-compatible mock: 200 + SSE end_turn on first request, 429 on second.
Bespoke recorder: launches mock, drives claude under --bare with mocked endpoint, captures lifecycle via attached --record daemon.
Feature: Token / quota budget exhausted L180-188. Three Expected: bullets all observable in this shape.

preconditions: go on PATH (to build the mock)
irrlichd running with --record (recording is captured via attached daemon)
setup: tools/agent-onboarding/recording/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh builds the mock, launches it, drives claude under --bare, and stages the fixture under .build/refresh/claudecode/token-quota-exhausted-<TS>/.
verify: events.jsonl shows session_birth → prompt1 working/ready arc → prompt2 working/ready arc
final state of the session is ready
no state_transition to waiting (no permission gates expected)
the second prompt's working→ready transition is the quota-exhausted code path
